result.stdout:
Usage: cosmos container delete-item [--compress-json] [--show-stats] [--partition-key <String>] [--consistency-level <String>] [--exclude-region <String>...] [--post-triggers <String>...] [--pre-triggers <String>...] [--session-token <String>] [--container <String>] [--database <String>] [--endpoint <String>] [--key <String>] [--verbose] [--help] json-items

Arguments:
  0: json-items    

Options:
  -j, --compress-json                 Compress the json output by removing whitespace and carriage returns.
  -s, --show-stats                    Show response statistics (including RUs, StatusCode, ContinuationToken).
  -p, --partition-key <String>        The name of the partition key property in the input JSON.
  -l, --consistency-level <String>    Consistency Level (Eventual, ConsistentPrefix, Session, BoundedStaleness, Strong)
  -r, --exclude-region <String>...    Exclude region(s) while querying
  --post-triggers <String>...         Set the names of the post trigger to be invoked after the upsert.
  --pre-triggers <String>...          Set the names of the pre trigger to be invoked after the upsert.
  --session-token <String>            Set a session token in the query request.
  -c, --container <String>            The name of the container that you are connecting to in Cosmos DB.
  -d, --database <String>             The name of the database that you are creating.
  -e, --endpoint <String>             The endpointUri used to connect to the Cosmos DB service
  -k, --key <String>                  The primary or secondary key used to connect to the Cosmos DB service.
  -v, --verbose                       Display more details of what is happening.
  -h, --help                          Show help message

result.stdout:
Usage: cosmos container new [--partition-key <String>] [--autoscale <Int32>] [--manual <Int32>] [--index <String>] [--vector <String>] [--default-time-to-live <Int32>] [--container <String>] [--database <String>] [--endpoint <String>] [--key <String>] [--verbose] [--help]

Options:
  -p, --partition-key <String>      Specify the name of the partition key.
  --autoscale <Int32>               (optional) set the number of autoscaled RU/s throughput.
  --manual <Int32>                  (optional) set the number of manual RU/s throughput.
  --index <String>                  (optional) file that holds the index policy.
  --vector <String>                 (optional) file that holds the vector policy.
  --default-time-to-live <Int32>    The time to live on the container.
  -c, --container <String>          The name of the container that you are connecting to in Cosmos DB.
  -d, --database <String>           The name of the database that you are creating.
  -e, --endpoint <String>           The endpointUri used to connect to the Cosmos DB service
  -k, --key <String>                The primary or secondary key used to connect to the Cosmos DB service.
  -v, --verbose                     Display more details of what is happening.
  -h, --help                        Show help message

result.stdout:
Usage: cosmos container select-item [--drop-system-properties] [--compress-json] [--show-stats] [--max-enumerations <Int32>] [--continuation-token-file <String>] [--consistency-level <String>] [--enable-low-precision-order-by] [--enable-optimistic-direct-execution] [--enable-scan-in-query] [--exclude-region <String>...] [--max-items <Int32>] [--populate-index-metrics] [--response-continuation-token-limit-in-kb <Int32>] [--session-token <String>] [--container <String>] [--database <String>] [--endpoint <String>] [--key <String>] [--verbose] [--help] query

Arguments:
  0: query    

Options:
  -_, --drop-system-properties                         Drop system generated properties (_rid, _attachments, _etag, _self, _ts, _lsn).
  -j, --compress-json                                  Compress the json output by removing whitespace and carriage returns.
  -s, --show-stats                                     Show response statistics (including RUs, StatusCode, ContinuationToken).
  -i, --max-enumerations <Int32>                       The maximum number of enumerations (Default is 1). (Default: 1)
  -t, --continuation-token-file <String>               File that holds the continuation token.
  -l, --consistency-level <String>                     Consistency Level (Eventual, ConsistentPrefix, Session, BoundedStaleness, Strong)
  --enable-low-precision-order-by                      Set low precision order by in your Azure Cosmos DB query.
  --enable-optimistic-direct-execution                 Disable direct (optimistic) execution of the query.
  --enable-scan-in-query                               Enable scans on the queries.
  -r, --exclude-region <String>...                     Exclude region(s) while querying
  -n, --max-items <Int32>                              The maximum of items in an enumeration
  -m, --populate-index-metrics                         Obtain which existing indexes where used and potential new indexes
  --response-continuation-token-limit-in-kb <Int32>    The response continuation token limit in KB request option for document query requests.
  --session-token <String>                             Set a session token in the query request.
  -c, --container <String>                             The name of the container that you are connecting to in Cosmos DB.
  -d, --database <String>                              The name of the database that you are creating.
  -e, --endpoint <String>                              The endpointUri used to connect to the Cosmos DB service
  -k, --key <String>                                   The primary or secondary key used to connect to the Cosmos DB service.
  -v, --verbose                                        Display more details of what is happening.
  -h, --help                                           Show help message

commands.payload:
Usage: cosmos container select-item [--drop-system-properties] [--compress-json] [--show-stats] [--max-enumerations <Int32>] [--continuation-token-file <String>] [--consistency-level <String>] [--enable-low-precision-order-by] [--enable-optimistic-direct-execution] [--enable-scan-in-query] [--exclude-region <String>...] [--max-items <Int32>] [--populate-index-metrics] [--response-continuation-token-limit-in-kb <Int32>] [--session-token <String>] [--container <String>] [--database <String>] [--endpoint <String>] [--key <String>] [--verbose] [--help] query

Arguments:
  0: query    

Options:
  -_, --drop-system-properties                         Drop system generated properties (_rid, _attachments, _etag, _self, _ts, _lsn).
  -j, --compress-json                                  Compress the json output by removing whitespace and carriage returns.
  -s, --show-stats                                     Show response statistics (including RUs, StatusCode, ContinuationToken).
  -i, --max-enumerations <Int32>                       The maximum number of enumerations (Default is 1). (Default: 1)
  -t, --continuation-token-file <String>               File that holds the continuation token.
  -l, --consistency-level <String>                     Consistency Level (Eventual, ConsistentPrefix, Session, BoundedStaleness, Strong)
  --enable-low-precision-order-by                      Set low precision order by in your Azure Cosmos DB query.
  --enable-optimistic-direct-execution                 Disable direct (optimistic) execution of the query.
  --enable-scan-in-query                               Enable scans on the queries.
  -r, --exclude-region <String>...                     Exclude region(s) while querying
  -n, --max-items <Int32>                              The maximum of items in an enumeration
  -m, --populate-index-metrics                         Obtain which existing indexes where used and potential new indexes
  --response-continuation-token-limit-in-kb <Int32>    The response continuation token limit in KB request option for document query requests.
  --session-token <String>                             Set a session token in the query request.
  -c, --container <String>                             The name of the container that you are connecting to in Cosmos DB.
  -d, --database <String>                              The name of the database that you are creating.
  -e, --endpoint <String>                              The endpointUri used to connect to the Cosmos DB service
  -k, --key <String>                                   The primary or secondary key used to connect to the Cosmos DB service.
